question: Which product category has the highest total revenue?
gold_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price), 2) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 1

question: Who are the top 5 customers by total spending?
gold_sql: SELECT c.name, ROUND(SUM(o.total_amount), 2) AS total_spent FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 5
notes: Tests multi-table JOIN + WHERE filter + ORDER BY

question: What is the average order value per country?
gold_sql: SELECT c.country, ROUND(AVG(o.total_amount), 2) AS avg_order_value, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.country ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC
notes: Tests GROUP BY on joined column

question: Which products have an average rating below 3?
gold_sql: SELECT p.name, p.category, ROUND(AVG(r.rating), 2) AS avg_rating, COUNT(r.id) AS review_count FROM reviews r JOIN products p ON r.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.id, p.name, p.category HAVING AVG(r.rating) < 3 ORDER BY avg_rating

question: How many orders were placed each month in 2024?
gold_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', order_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders WHERE order_date LIKE '2024%' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

question: What percentage of orders are completed?
gold_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS count, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 1) AS percentage FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY count DESC

question: What are the top 3 best-selling products by quantity sold?
gold_sql: SELECT p.name, p.category, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.id, p.name, p.category ORDER BY total_quantity DESC LIMIT 3

question: Which customers have placed more than 5 orders?
gold_sql: SELECT c.name, c.segment, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name, c.segment HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 5 ORDER BY order_count DESC

question: Which city has the most Premium segment customers?
gold_sql: SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS premium_count FROM customers WHERE segment = 'Premium' GROUP BY city ORDER BY premium_count DESC LIMIT 1
notes: Tests WHERE on enum + GROUP BY

question: What is the total revenue and number of orders for each product category in 2024?
gold_sql: SELECT p.category, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS orders, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price), 2) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.order_date LIKE '2024%' GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY revenue DESC
